questions.question: What is reflection of light?
questions.correct_answer: The bouncing back of light when it strikes a surface
questions.explanation: When light hits a smooth surface like a mirror, it returns into the same medium instead of passing through.

questions.question: State the first law of reflection.
questions.correct_answer: The angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection
questions.explanation: This means that the incoming light ray and the reflected light ray make equal angles with the normal.

questions.question: State the second law of reflection.
questions.correct_answer: The incident ray, reflected ray, and the normal all lie in the same plane
questions.explanation: This ensures that reflection follows a predictable geometric path.

questions.question: What is a plane mirror?
questions.correct_answer: A mirror with a flat reflective surface
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors form virtual, erect, and same-sized images of objects.

questions.question: What type of image is formed by a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: The image cannot be obtained on a screen because it appears behind the mirror.

questions.question: What is refraction of light?
questions.correct_answer: The bending of light when it passes from one medium to another
questions.explanation: Refraction occurs due to the change in speed of light in different media.

questions.question: Why does refraction occur?
questions.correct_answer: Because the speed of light changes when it moves between different media
questions.explanation: Light travels at different speeds in air, water, glass, and other materials.

questions.question: What is the refractive index of a medium?
questions.correct_answer: The ratio of the speed of light in vacuum to the speed of light in the medium
questions.explanation: It indicates how much light slows down in a medium compared to vacuum.

questions.question: What happens when light passes from a rarer medium to a denser medium?
questions.explanation: This happens because light slows down in a denser medium.

questions.question: What happens when light passes from a denser medium to a rarer medium?
questions.correct_answer: It bends away from the normal
questions.explanation: This happens because light speeds up in a rarer medium.

notes.title: Reflection of Light
notes.content: The bouncing back of light into the same medium after striking a surface.

notes.title: Laws of Reflection
notes.content: 1) Angle of incidence = Angle of reflection. 2) Incident ray, reflected ray and normal lie in the same plane.

notes.title: Refraction of Light
notes.content: The bending of light when it passes from one medium to another due to change in speed.

notes.title: Refractive Index
notes.content: It measures how much light slows down in a medium compared to vacuum.

notes.title: Image in Plane Mirror
notes.content: Virtual, erect, same size as object, and laterally inverted.

facts.fact: Reflection follows two basic laws relating angles and the plane of rays.
Refraction occurs due to change in speed of light between media.
Light bends toward the normal when entering a denser medium.
Light bends away from the normal when entering a rarer medium.
Plane mirrors always form virtual images that cannot be captured on a screen.
